message:
test(command): CLUSTER COUNTKEYSINSLOT/GETKEYSINSLOT edge semantics (frankenredis-koy0)

Extends the cluster-disabled error-surface coverage in fr-command with the
parity edge cases that vendored Redis actually differentiates for
COUNTKEYSINSLOT and GETKEYSINSLOT, and mirrors the same labels in the live
differential smoke list so we run them against a real redis-server.

Cases added to the dispatch_argv error-surface assertion:

  COUNTKEYSINSLOT 0            — boundary slot (lowest valid)
  COUNTKEYSINSLOT 16384        — one past the top slot (Redis returns error)
  COUNTKEYSINSLOT abc          — non-integer slot (wrongtype-style error)
  GETKEYSINSLOT 0 10           — lowest valid slot with count
  GETKEYSINSLOT -1 10          — negative slot (error path, not clamp)
  GETKEYSINSLOT abc 10         — non-integer slot (parser error, not EINVAL)

And mirrored in core_cluster_disabled_surface_live_redis_matches_runtime:

  cluster_countkeysinslot_slot_zero
  cluster_countkeysinslot_non_integer
  cluster_countkeysinslot_negative_error
  cluster_countkeysinslot_invalid_16384
  cluster_getkeysinslot_non_integer_slot
  cluster_getkeysinslot_negative_slot_error
  cluster_getkeysinslot_slot_out_of_range

These close the last edge-condition gaps between our cluster-disabled error
surface and upstream Redis, so integer-parse vs range-check vs
wrong-subcommand-shape errors can't diverge silently. Also records the
in-progress frankenredis-koy0 issue in the beads snapshot for traceability.

 {"id":"frankenredis-klvg","title":"Forward BGREWRITEAOF from dispatch_argv to runtime via Store flag","description":"# Forward BGREWRITEAOF from dispatch_argv to runtime via Store flag

## Problem
BGREWRITEAOF in fr-command returns the success message without actually writing. The runtime has the real implementation. Lua redis.call(\"BGREWRITEAOF\") is a no-op.

## Design
Add a pub bgrewriteaof_requested: bool flag to Store. When fr-command's bgrewriteaof_cmd is called, set this flag to true and return the success message. The runtime checks this flag after each dispatch and performs the actual AOF rewrite.

## Implementation
1. Add pub bgrewriteaof_requested: bool to Store (default false)
2. fr-command bgrewriteaof_cmd sets store.bgrewriteaof_requested = true
3. Runtime checks store.bgrewriteaof_requested after execute_frame and calls persist_snapshot_to_disk
4. Clear the flag after handling

## Why this pattern
Same as DEBUG RELOAD — the fr-command layer can signal intent, the runtime fulfills it. This is the cleanest cross-layer communication without restructuring dispatch_argv's signature.

## Testing
- Integration test: verify AOF file is written when BGREWRITEAOF called from Lua
- Conformance: existing BGREWRITEAOF test should still pass

## Files to modify
- crates/fr-store/src/lib.rs (flag field)
- crates/fr-command/src/lib.rs (set flag)
- crates/fr-runtime/src/lib.rs (check flag, perform rewrite)","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:20:53.197611515Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-04T01:26:03.762153750Z","closed_at":"2026-04-04T01:26:03.761892425Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
